zh.lifeSummary: 當Ann Brown於1579年8月3日之後出生在英國盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆時，她的父親Richard Browne8452歲，母親Agnes Mary Eliza Wing8448歲。她於1595年與William Nicholas在英國結婚。她去世於1620年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可哈爾斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Brown
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Brunson
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Brun (Old English Brūna or Old Norse Brúni) + -son ‘Brown's son’. See Brown . There may also be occasional survivals of an Old English compound personal name Brūnsunu ‘brown son’, attested in the Domesday Book, and presumably given to a child with brown hair or complexion. Whether it was also used as an adult nickname is uncertain. This form of the surname is most common in Norfolk.

variants.name: Browning
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Brouning (Old English Brūning), a derivative of the byname Brūn (see Brown ).

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. William Browning was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Browne
meanings: English: variant of   Brown  .
Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Burnett
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Tyrone and Antrim): descriptive nickname of Norman origin, from Old French burnete, a diminutive of brun ‘brown’ (see Brown ), or possibly in some instances a metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of burnete, a high quality woolen cloth, originally dark brown in color.
Probably also an altered form of English   Barnett  .
In some cases also an altered form of French   Burnet  ; compare   Burnette  .

variants.name: Erjavec
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from an old spelling of rjavec, most commonly meaning ‘dark skinned man’ and ‘brown horse’. Compare Brown .

variants.name: Borron
meanings: Scottish: perhaps a variant of   Brown   in a Scottish pronunciation. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Broun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Lyubov, Sergey.
Scottish: variant of   Brown  .
Jewish: variant of   Braun  .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Brown was born after 3 August 1579, in North Luffenham, Rutland, England, her father, Richard Browne, was 8452 and her mother, Agnes Mary Eliza Wing, was 8448. She married William Nicholas in 1595, in England. She died in 1620, in Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Ann Brown(이)가 1579년 8월 3일 이후 영국 루틀랜드 노르스 루펜함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Browne(은)는 8452세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Mary Eliza Wing(은)는 8448세였다. 그녀는 1595년 영국에서 William Nicholas(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1620년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 찰레스타운에서 사망했다.

ru.Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Ba: чарльстоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ann Brown родилась после 3 августа 1579 г. в местности норт луффенам, рутланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Browne было 8452 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Mary Eliza Wing, 8448. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Nicholas в 1595 г. в местности Англия. Она умерла в 1620 г. в местности чарльстоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ann Brown nació después de 3 de agosto de 1579, en North Luffenham, Rutland, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Browne, tenía 8452 años y su mamá, Agnes Mary Eliza Wing, tenía 8448 años. Se casó con William Nicholas en 1595, en Inglaterra. Murió en 1620, en Charlestown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
